title: 设置虚拟机备注 (VSSD.Notes)
code:
$ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'
$ns = 'root\virtualization\v2'
function Wait-Job2($p){ if(-not $p){ return 7 }; $j = [wmi]$p; while($j.JobState -eq 3 -or $j.JobState -eq 4){ Start-Sleep -Milliseconds 200; $j = [wmi]$p }; return $j.JobState }
$vsms = Get-WmiObject -Namespace $ns -Class Msvm_VirtualSystemManagementService
# 获取目标虚拟机的设置数据（$vm 为该虚拟机的 Msvm_ComputerSystem）
$vssd = $vm.GetRelated('Msvm_VirtualSystemSettingData') | Select-Object -First 1
$vssd = [wmi]$vssd.__PATH
# Notes 在 v2 中为数组类型，但 Hyper-V 仅保留首个元素，故使用单元素数组
$vssd.Notes = @('My note text (multi-word / multi-line is fine in one element)')
$rm = $vsms.ModifySystemSettings($vssd.GetText(1))
if($rm.ReturnValue -eq 4096){ Wait-Job2 $rm.Job | Out-Null }
elseif($rm.ReturnValue -ne 0){ throw "ModifySystemSettings rv=$($rm.ReturnValue)" }
# 读回
$readNotes = @( ([wmi]$vm.__PATH).GetRelated('Msvm_VirtualSystemSettingData') | Select-Object -First 1 ).Notes
Write-Host ($readNotes -join ' | ')
output:
READBACK Notes count = 1
READBACK[0] = WMITEST note set via WMI
ASSERT PASS set_notes Notes=[WMITEST note set via WMI]
CLEANUP DestroySystem rv=4096
LEFTOVER count = 0
notes: 修改备注属于整机设置：获取虚拟机的 Msvm_VirtualSystemSettingData，设置其 .Notes，再调用 $vsms.ModifySystemSettings($vssd.GetText(1))；不应使用 ModifyResourceSettings。
VSSD.Notes 在 v2 命名空间 schema 中为 string[]（array=true），但 Hyper-V 实现仅保留数组的第一个元素：传入 @('a','b') 读回仅剩 'a'。可靠写法是使用单元素数组 @('整段备注')，多行或多词内容放在同一字符串中即可。
ModifySystemSettings 可能同步返回 rv=0（未触发 4096 job），仍应保留 Wait-Job2 作为兜底。
创建实例时不能使用 [wmiclass]"\\.\$ns:Class" 强制转换（会报无效参数），应改用 (Get-WmiObject -Namespace $ns -Class <Class> -List).CreateInstance()。另注意 PowerShell 会将 "$ns:" 解析为作用域变量，需写作 "${ns}:"。
notes_en: Modifying the notes is a whole-system setting: obtain the virtual machine's Msvm_VirtualSystemSettingData, set its .Notes, then call $vsms.ModifySystemSettings($vssd.GetText(1)); ModifyResourceSettings should not be used.

VSSD.Notes is declared as string[] (array=true) in the v2 namespace schema, but the Hyper-V implementation retains only the first element of the array: passing @('a','b') reads back as only 'a'. The reliable approach is to use a single-element array @('the entire note'), placing multi-line or multi-word content within that one string.
ModifySystemSettings may return rv=0 synchronously (without raising a 4096 job); Wait-Job2 should still be kept as a fallback.

When creating an instance, the [wmiclass]"\\.\$ns:Class" cast cannot be used (it reports an invalid parameter); use (Get-WmiObject -Namespace $ns -Class <Class> -List).CreateInstance() instead. Note also that PowerShell parses "$ns:" as a scoped variable, so it must be written as "${ns}:".
code_en:
$ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'
$ns = 'root\virtualization\v2'
function Wait-Job2($p){ if(-not $p){ return 7 }; $j = [wmi]$p; while($j.JobState -eq 3 -or $j.JobState -eq 4){ Start-Sleep -Milliseconds 200; $j = [wmi]$p }; return $j.JobState }
$vsms = Get-WmiObject -Namespace $ns -Class Msvm_VirtualSystemManagementService
# Get the setting data of the target virtual machine ($vm is the Msvm_ComputerSystem of that virtual machine)
$vssd = $vm.GetRelated('Msvm_VirtualSystemSettingData') | Select-Object -First 1
$vssd = [wmi]$vssd.__PATH
# Notes is an array type in v2, but Hyper-V keeps only the first element, so use a single-element array
$vssd.Notes = @('My note text (multi-word / multi-line is fine in one element)')
$rm = $vsms.ModifySystemSettings($vssd.GetText(1))
if($rm.ReturnValue -eq 4096){ Wait-Job2 $rm.Job | Out-Null }
elseif($rm.ReturnValue -ne 0){ throw "ModifySystemSettings rv=$($rm.ReturnValue)" }
# Read back
$readNotes = @( ([wmi]$vm.__PATH).GetRelated('Msvm_VirtualSystemSettingData') | Select-Object -First 1 ).Notes
Write-Host ($readNotes -join ' | ')
